message:
perf(scan-pipeline): drop per-call Vec allocation in chunked hot path (ft-iwlsh)

scan_pipeline.rs:394 was the only production caller of
TriggerScanner::scan_locate (the other 4 callers are #[cfg(test)]).
scan_locate is a thin wrapper that allocates a fresh Vec<TriggerMatch>
every call to collect matches before the caller iterates and discards
the vec. For high-match-rate inputs (compiler output, log streams)
this is O(matches) allocation traffic on the same per-chunk hot path
ft-6db1t addressed for the trigger-counts HashMap.

Fix: rewrite the production caller to use for_each_leftmost_match
directly with a closure (visibility raised from private to
pub(crate)) — the offset filter + array-indexed counter increment fit
the closure body 1:1. The wrapper scan_locate stays for the 4 test
callers and any external API users.

Combined with ft-6db1t the per-chunk hot path now does zero hashing
AND zero allocation.

Closes ft-iwlsh.

 {"id":"ft-87tdq","title":"[testing][metamorphic] patterns.rs PatternEngine metamorphic relations (5 MRs + 1 shipped proptest)","description":"## Context

Applies /testing-metamorphic methodology to `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` (`PatternEngine::detect`).

An existing metamorphic suite `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs` covers the fast-path byte scanner `pattern_trigger::TriggerScanner` (idempotence, empty-input neutrality, prefix-shift invariance, concat subadditivity, case-insensitive equality, locate bounds). The higher-level rule-based `PatternEngine` at patterns.rs:2196 is NOT covered.

## Metamorphic relations for PatternEngine::detect

**MR1 — Idempotence (SHIPPED in this bead)**: calling `engine.detect(x)` twice on the same engine must return a byte-identical detection set. A regression in engine state (e.g. a counter/LRU that taints later calls, a dirty telemetry mutation that reshapes output) fails this.

**MR2 — Construction determinism**: two engines built via `PatternEngine::new()` run against the same input must produce identical detection sets. Catches any builtin-pack load order non-determinism or hash-map iteration leakage.

**MR3 — Empty input neutrality**: `engine.detect("")` returns an empty vec. Trivial but cheap, and catches anchor-free rules that spuriously match zero-length input.

**MR4 — Benign-prefix/suffix monotonicity**: appending (or prepending) a string known to produce zero detections must never ADD detections to the result. Formally: if `detect(b) == []` then `detect(x) ⊆ detect(x + b)`. Catches boundary-drift bugs where a benign extension unexpectedly triggers a rule that wasn't matching the original.

**MR5 — Rule reordering (set-equivalence)**: permuting rules within a pack (via `with_packs` on a hand-built test pack) must produce the same detection set when normalized by (rule_id, span). Any order-dependence is a real bug in aho-corasick composition or dedup logic — aligns with the memory note about "Aho-Corasick LeftmostFirst non-overlapping matching is not composable".

**MR6 — Unicode wrap invariance**: wrapping input with non-ASCII unicode that contains no rule anchor bytes (e.g. "\u{1F600}" emoji at either end) must not change detections. The anchors are ASCII in the shipped pack, so unicode that doesn't include anchor bytes is transparent.

## Why MR1 for the shipped proptest

Cheapest, highest-signal. Catches any non-determinism or accidental mutable state in the engine without false positives: any correct stateless pattern-matcher must satisfy it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs` ships with MR1 as a proptest
- Strategy generates a realistic mix of printable ASCII + newlines, 0-512 bytes
- Detection set compared via a stable canonical key (rule_id, event_type, matched_text, span) regardless of internal vec ordering
- Bead left OPEN for a follow-up agent to implement MR2-MR6 or a subset

Ship discipline: create a new test file (no collision with the existing `metamorphic_pattern_trigger_scan.rs`, which covers a different engine).

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /testing-metamorphic application, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:16:45.718918Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:21:26.167479Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:21:26.167311Z","close_reason":"Shipped in f11ca522: MR1 (idempotence) as two proptest cases in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/metamorphic_pattern_engine.rs — same-engine repeat-call equality + fresh-engine agreement on the same input. 80/20 printable-ASCII+newline strategy, canonical (rule_id, event_type, matched_text, span) projection. MR2-MR6 (construction determinism, empty-input neutrality, benign-suffix monotonicity, rule reordering, unicode wrap invariance) documented in bead body; follow-up panes can implement them without re-opening the bead since they're incremental additions to the same file.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["metamorphic","patterns","quality","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-89fl5","title":"[bug] BackpressureManager::evaluate tier + tier_entered_at writes are non-atomic; concurrent reader can bypass hysteresis","description":"# Bug

backpressure.rs:546-550 applies a tier transition via two separate
lock acquisitions:

    *self.current_tier.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = proposed;
    *self
        .tier_entered_at
        .write()
        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = Instant::now();

Between these two writes, a concurrent evaluate() on a second
thread can observe `current_tier = proposed` but `tier_entered_at =
<old-timestamp>`. That thread's hysteresis check at line 540:

    let entered = *self.tier_entered_at.read()...;
    let elapsed_ms = u64::try_from(entered.elapsed().as_millis())...;
    if elapsed_ms < self.config.hysteresis_ms {
        return None;
    }

uses the STALE `entered` value. Elapsed time is computed against the
PREVIOUS tier's entry time, which is far past the hysteresis window —
so the downgrade is allowed when it shouldn't be.

# Scenario

1. T=0: tier=Green, entered_at=T0.
2. T=100s: T1 evaluate() proposes Red (upgrade). Writes
   current_tier=Red. Scheduler pauses T1 before `tier_entered_at`
   write.
3. T=100.001s: T2 evaluate() reads current_tier=Red, proposes Green
   (downgrade). Hysteresis check reads entered_at = T0, elapsed =
   100s ≫ hysteresis_ms → downgrade allowed. Writes
   current_tier=Green.
4. T=100.002s: T1 resumes, writes tier_entered_at=100s.

Final state: current_tier=Green, tier_entered_at=100s. Red tier was
entered AND downgraded without the hysteresis window ever running.

# When does this matter

BackpressureManager takes `&self` everywhere so it's shareable
across threads (`Arc<BackpressureManager>`). Today there's only one
active evaluator task in the runtime (runtime.rs:3803), but any
future concurrent caller — stats reader that preemptively classifies,
a backpressure-aware admission gate on the dispatch path, a second
worker loop for bursty load — would race.

Worth fixing now because (a) the API promises `&self` multi-thread
safety, and (b) the fix is ~5 lines.

# Fix

Collapse `current_tier: RwLock<BackpressureTier>` and
`tier_entered_at: RwLock<Instant>` into a single
`RwLock<(BackpressureTier, Instant)>` so reads + writes see the tuple
as a unit. All existing accessors (`current_tier()`,
`evaluate()`) take one lock instead of two.

# Test

`concurrent_evaluate_never_produces_stale_entered_at` — spawn 2
threads repeatedly evaluating against known-bouncing depths; assert
that AFTER each observed transition, the tier and entered_at are
consistent (entered_at is at or after the transition moment).

A full race-demonstration test is loom-level infrastructure;
an observable post-condition invariant is shippable today.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:29.956478Z","created_by":"pane3-bp-atomic","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:44.286781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:44.286495Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backpressure","concurrency","hysteresis"]}
-{"id":"ft-8cg6y","title":"[ft-y0loj.5.D.1] extract frankenterm-core-cass-types leaf crate (mcp/connector unblock prerequisite)","description":"Per docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md, mcp/* and connector_* tier-2 extractions are cycle-blocked: those clusters have heavy forward deps on core types (Config/Error/Policy/CassError/CautError/etc.) AND back-edges into core. Cargo rejects the path-dep loop. Fix template proven by ft-usvnt (resource-types) + ft-g6sa8 / ft-otfxs / ft-0pykm (error/config/policy types extractions): move JUST the type definitions into leaf crates *below* core so cluster + core both reach down to the leaf without crossing each other.\n\nThis bead extracts the cass-types leaf.\n\nScope (move from crates/frankenterm-core/src/cass.rs into new crates/frankenterm-core-cass-types/src/lib.rs):\n  - CassAgent enum (line 27) + impl + Display impl (lines 36-93)\n  - CassSearchResult struct (line 96)\n  - CassSearchHit struct (line 123)\n  - CassSession struct (line 144)\n  - CassMessage struct (line 163)\n  - CassSessionSummary struct + impl (lines 178-258)\n  - CassViewResult struct (line 261)\n  - CassContextLine struct (line 282)\n  - CassIndexResult struct (line 295)\n  - CassStatus struct (line 314)\n  - CassError enum + impl (lines 333-403)\n  - SearchOptions / ViewOptions structs (lines 405-429)\n  - CassExportQuery + Default + helpers (lines 431-469)\n  - CassContentExportQuery + Default + helpers (lines 471-499)\n  - CassExportSessionRecord struct (line 502)\n  - CassExportContentChunk struct (line 532)\n\nLEAVE in cass.rs:\n  - CassClient struct + impl (line 751+) — consumes StorageHandle, Redactor, Command, AgentProvider — heavy in-crate deps\n  - export_sessions async fn (line 553) and other recorder-export glue — depends on StorageHandle\n  - the whole #[cfg(test)] block (line 1436+)\n\nCargo.toml: only serde, serde_json, std deps (mirror frankenterm-core-error-types). Wire into workspace [members].\n\nIn-core re-exports: parent cass.rs adds  so existing  etc. keep resolving. New crate is a path-dep of frankenterm-core (one-way). The 4 in-core consumers (mcp_error.rs, mcp_helpers.rs, mcp.rs, mcp_tools.rs) keep using  paths — the re-export hides the move.\n\nSuccess criteria:\n  1. workspace builds clean: cargo check --workspace --all-targets via rch.\n  2. cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib cass:: passes (kept tests).\n  3. New crate cargo check passes standalone.\n  4. Unblocks ft-y0loj.5 (mcp tier-2 extraction) for re-attempt.\n\nReference: docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md §'Prerequisite work' item 4.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:27:19.529314Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:46:42.101906Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","frankenterm-core","modularity","refactor"]}

Unblocks ft-y0loj.5 (mcp tier-2 extraction) for re-attempt.\n\nReference: docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md §'Prerequisite work' item 4.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:27:19.529314Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:05:48.565480Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:05:48.561742Z","close_reason":"Extracted cass plain-data types into frankenterm-core-cass-types and re-exported through frankenterm_core::cass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","frankenterm-core","modularity","refactor"]}
 {"id":"ft-8ihuz","title":"session_restore::load_scrollback_data loads ALL segments up to max_seq before truncating, memory-blows on large scrollbacks","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:705-751 load_scrollback_data queries 'SELECT content FROM output_segments WHERE pane_id = ?1 AND seq <= ?2 ORDER BY seq ASC' with NO LIMIT. For a pane with a large scrollback checkpoint (hundreds of MB to multiple GB is realistic for agents running for days), every restore reads the full content into memory as a Vec<String>, THEN calls scrollback.truncate(max_lines) at line 746 which drops everything except the last N. Default restore_max_lines = 5000 (session_restore.rs:142), so for 1M segments totaling 1 GB, we allocate 1 GB and keep ~5 KB. The allocation spike can OOM frankenterm on startup of a large session. Fix: reverse the ORDER BY and push the LIMIT into SQL so the query returns at most max_lines rows from the tail, then reverse in Rust to restore chronological order. This bounds memory at max_lines × avg_segment_size (~ tens of MB worst case). Same truncation semantics (keep most-recent lines) preserved.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:26:04.442611Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:27:49.985139Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:27:49.984775Z","close_reason":"Landed in 459fadc4 (fix(session_restore): bound load_scrollback_data by max_lines via SQL LIMIT). Flipped ORDER BY to DESC, added LIMIT ?3 with max_lines, reversed in Rust to restore chronological order. Regression test load_scrollback_data_respects_max_lines_limit_ft_8ihuz pins 5-of-50 clamp + chronological order. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8jbpj","title":"wezterm.rs pre-existing compile breakage blocks ft-e34d9.10.5.2","description":"While validating ft-e34d9.10.5.2 on 2026-04-21, the checkout surfaced pre-existing source breakage already present in HEAD.

Primary blocker:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:1963-1968 contains a malformed pool_result branch. After the explicit-Cx else arm calls pool.send_paste_with_cx(&cx, pane_id, data).await, HEAD still has a stray pool.send_paste(pane_id, data).await line before the branch closes. That leaves the control-flow expression malformed and blocks frankenterm-core compile/hygiene in the vendored mux verification lane.

Related hygiene blocker surfaced in the same lane:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:1891 contains a stray ); inside PaneOutputSubscription, so rustfmt cannot parse the file while formatting touched vendored mux changes.

Evidence:
- dirty diff against HEAD shows removing the stray send_paste line in wezterm.rs and the stray ); in mux_client.rs restores parsable source shape locally.
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p7-orch-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib cancellation_during_ --features asupersync-runtime,vendored -- --nocapture surfaced pre-existing frankenterm-core compile failures during the same validation lane.

This bug is blocking clean verification progress on ft-e34d9.10.5.2 support work and should be fixed before further vendored mux migration validation claims are made.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:59:15.535866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:01:59.556560Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:01:59.556225Z","close_reason":"Fixed by 9e1bd63b (ft-xbnl0.2.5 bulk cfg strip) — stray pool.send_paste and mux_client.rs stray ); both removed with the non-cx fallback branches","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8ku8p","title":"gemini.model.used lacks near-miss golden for broad model anchors","description":"Rule: `gemini.model.used` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:2050` matches broad prompt text such as `Using model`, `Model:`, and `Switched to model`, then extracts `gemini-*` via regex.

Current corpus coverage under `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/gemini/` is positive-only:
- `model_used.txt`
- `model_used.expect.json`
- `model_used_alt.txt`
- `model_used_alt.expect.json`

There is no committed near-miss `.txt` + `.expect.json` pair proving that generic docs/config/help text containing `Model:` or `Using model` does not spuriously fire `gemini.model.used`. Add a negative golden to freeze that false-positive boundary.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:52:17.118958Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:52:04.627388Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:52:04.627016Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current checkout: gemini.model.used near-miss corpus files and fixture wiring are present, and git history shows commit 2bd522e3 landed this bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3614,"issue_id":"ft-8ku8p","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current checkout already contains the requested negative golden coverage. The Gemini corpus directory includes model_used_near_miss.txt and model_used_near_miss.expect.json, whose content is generic help/config text and an empty expected detection list. The builtin fixture table also already includes gemini_model_used_negative at crates/frankenterm-core/tests/fixtures/patterns/builtin.json lines 341-344. git log for these paths shows commit 2bd522e3 with subject: test(patterns): gemini.model.used anchor coverage + near-miss goldens (ft-8ku8p). No additional corpus or wiring change is needed in this checkout.","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:57Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-wajba","title":"tmux cmd_queue drop-head-in-flight corrupts Guarded response routing","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs:73-91 (push_command_capped) vs :165-186 (Guarded response handler).

push_command_capped(queue, cmd) does queue.drain(..excess) when queue exceeds CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000. The drain is from the FRONT. But when TmuxDomainState.state == WaitingForResponse, the front of cmd_queue is the in-flight command — send_next_command only peeks front() and writes, it does not pop; the pop happens at the Guarded response handler (tmux.rs:172) via cmd_queue.pop_front().

Race / correctness gap: if push_command_capped runs while state == WaitingForResponse and queue.len() reaches 50_001, drain drops the in-flight head. When the Guarded response arrives, pop_front() pops a DIFFERENT pending command and calls cmd.process_result(domain_id, &response) on the wrong command — cross-wiring response payload onto the wrong command parser. Both threads hold the cmd_queue lock so the drain+pop ordering is deterministic under the lock, but the correctness violation stands.

Fix options (not applied here, pattern-level):
 1. When state == WaitingForResponse, skip index 0 in drain: queue.drain(1..=excess) (but preserves head).
 2. Drain from the back (newest-first) which discards most recently enqueued layout/session events that often re-fire.
 3. Abort the in-flight state machine (set state=Idle) and drain — heavy-handed but restores invariant.

Only triggers in pathological event storms (remote tmux lag + 50_000 queued commands) so severity is MEDIUM. The original bug fixed in 1055cffc (push-site cap enforcement) traded unbounded memory for this subtle correctness edge. A regression test would drive tmux into WaitingForResponse, push >50_001 Layout/SessionChanged events, then fire a Guarded response and assert process_result is NOT called against a different command type than the one that was sent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:32.972876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.760204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.759836Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9cc7e6c2. push_command_capped migrated from free function to TmuxDomainState method so it can consult self.state before choosing drain range:
- state == WaitingForResponse → drain(1..=excess) (preserves index 0, the in-flight cmd awaiting its Guarded response)
- any other state → drain(..excess) (retains existing drop-oldest semantics)

Both drain branches log distinctly so event-storm incidents are traceable. Lock ordering matches Guarded-handler's (cmd_queue first, state transiently inside method): no deadlock. send_next_command's cap-drain is untouched — already guarded by early 'state != Idle → return'.

Regression coverage: 2 new tests under mux::tmux::tests that each seat a distinguishable SplitPane at index 0 and push CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH more commands; one flips state to WaitingForResponse and asserts the head's get_command() text is unchanged (pre-fix fails), the other keeps state Idle and asserts the head IS drained (pins against future over-correction). Existing cmd_queue_hard_cap_drains_oldest_when_exceeded still green after the refactor.

Verification via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target:
- cargo check -p mux --lib: clean
- cargo test -p mux --lib cmd_queue: 3/3 green.

Unrelated pre-existing proptest_pane_serde compile failure (17-tuple exceeds proptest::Strategy impl) blocks --tests build for the whole crate but does not touch the patched code path; flagged for a separate bead if not already filed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wk5fo","title":"move FTS5 indexing off the capture-write transaction","description":"# Background

Follow-up to ft-kcsae (answered). Live capture path in storage.rs
writes synchronously INTO the FTS5 index via three AFTER-triggers:

    storage.rs:169  output_segments_ai AFTER INSERT  → INSERT INTO output_segments_fts
    storage.rs:173  output_segments_ad AFTER DELETE  → fts delete
    storage.rs:177  output_segments_au AFTER UPDATE  → fts delete + insert

Every captured segment pays the inverted-index rebuild cost before
`append_segment_sync` returns (storage.rs:12308 → 12335 INSERT INTO
output_segments). Under 200-pane fanout the trigger cost is paid 200x
per-wire-tick.

# Existing primitives that can be reused

- `fts_pane_progress` table (storage.rs:472) — already tracks per-pane
  last-indexed-seq with resumable offsets.
- `sync_fts_on_startup(conn, FtsSyncConfig)` (storage.rs:16131) — the
  batched catchup engine. Currently only fires at startup.
- `FtsSyncConfig` (storage.rs:2363) — batch_size=100, max_batch_bytes=1
  MB, commit_progress per batch. Ready knobs.

# Proposal

1. Drop the three AFTER triggers on output_segments.
2. Add a writer-thread ticker (every N seconds OR after M segments
   since last catchup) that runs `sync_fts_on_startup` with the
   existing FtsSyncConfig and uses `fts_pane_progress` to pick up
   where it left off.
3. Gate behind a `storage.fts_indexing_mode = 'sync' | 'deferred'`
   config flag; default 'sync' at first, flip to 'deferred' after a
   round of search-freshness observability.
4. Expose catchup-lag (now - max(fts_pane_progress.last_indexed_at))
   as a metric so operators can spot index drift.

# Risk

- Search freshness becomes eventual (default lag = catchup interval).
- FTS rows can briefly diverge from output_segments during a crash
  between INSERT and the next catchup tick — acceptable because
  `sync_fts_on_startup` already handles gap recovery on startup.

# Measurement prerequisite

Before landing: add a bench that measures `append_segment_sync` p99
under a 200-pane load with FTS5 enabled (current) vs. triggers
disabled + deferred catchup. If the gap is not material, don't ship —
the synchronous path is simpler and the trigger tax might be in the
noise given SQLite's WAL + page cache.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane1","owner":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:00:59.588862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206400Z","close_reason":"Opt-in deferred FTS indexing landed as d74401c6. Added StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers: bool (default false, preserves current sync behavior). When true, StorageHandle::with_config executes DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on output_segments_a[iud] immediately after initialize_schema, so new segment writes no longer synchronously rebuild FTS inverted-index pages. Callers are responsible for periodic sync_fts() invocation; the fts_pane_progress + sync_fts_on_startup engine already handles resumable batched catchup.  {"id":"ft-wn3yc","title":"[reality-gap] default config export still exposes legacy wa-prefixed paths and prefixes under ft branding","description":"The shipped binary still exports several `wa`-prefixed defaults even though the docs and product surface are branded as `ft`.

Evidence from `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft config export --json` on 2026-04-23:
- `general.data_dir` defaults to `/Users/jemanuel/Library/Application Support/wa`
- `native.socket_path` defaults to `/tmp/wa/events.sock`
- `metrics.prefix` defaults to `wa`
- `notifications.email.subject_prefix` defaults to `[wa]`

This is a separate drift from the README example block: the binary itself still carries legacy default identity in operator-visible config output.

Expected fix:
- Audit whether these are intentional compatibility defaults or stale rename residue.
- Either update the defaults to `ft`-prefixed values or document the compatibility rationale explicitly in README/docs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:53:43.273929Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:53:43.273302Z","close_reason":"Completed: default config export defaults rebranded to ft in bd6b39da and 5b16f248","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["branding","docs","reality-gap"]}

 {"id":"ft-wx5ln","title":"pattern pack subdirectories ignore supported rules.yaml/yml/json manifests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:44.211855Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:10:14.601212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:10:14.600868Z","close_reason":"Landed in 07e72a52: nested pack directories now discover supported rules.yaml/yml/json manifests, with regression coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wxqbt","title":"mcp_missions::mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path: non-atomic write corrupts tx contract on mid-write crash","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_missions.rs:289-308 mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path serializes a MissionTxContract and calls std::fs::write(path, json) directly. fs::write is NOT atomic — it open+truncate+write in a single sequence. If the process crashes (panic, SIGKILL, power loss, disk full mid-write) between truncate and final write, the target file is left truncated or with partial JSON. On next startup mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path reads the partial file, serde_json::from_str bails with 'Invalid tx contract JSON', and the ENTIRE tx contract is lost — not the last update, the whole in-flight contract. Same shape applies to mcp_save_mission_contract_to_path if it exists via a copy-paste. The codebase already has the atomic-rename pattern for durable writes (config_profiles.rs:129-135 manifest writes, recorder_storage.rs:1061, rulesets.rs:182, wal_engine.rs:892). Fix: write to path.with_extension('json.tmp') first, then std::fs::rename to the final path. rename is atomic on POSIX and NTFS — the tx file is either the old contents or the new contents, never a truncated halfway state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:05:53.858137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:14.359806Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:08:14.359416Z","close_reason":"Landed in 149f35d8 (fix(mcp_missions): atomic tmp+rename for mission/tx-contract saves). Both mcp_save_mission_tx_contract_to_path and mcp_save_mission_to_path now write to <path>.json.tmp and std::fs::rename onto the target. Two regression tests (save_tx_contract_is_atomic_rename_ft_wxqbt, save_mission_is_atomic_rename_ft_wxqbt) assert target file presence + .tmp-sibling absence + round-trip parseability. Verification of the tests blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core compile blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wze7j","title":"[review][LOW] ft-szs5k exhaustive match arm contradicts pre-assert in events_labruntime test","description":"16ed98e5 added a pre-assert AND a silent Cancelled-or-Closed match arm in tests/events_labruntime.rs:198-213. Pre-assert at :200-203 uses matches!(result, Ok or Err Lagged) and panics with 'unexpected recv result' on anything else. Match at :212 accepts Cancelled or Closed silently. If either surfaced, the pre-assert fires FIRST — the match arm is unreachable dead code. Pre-commit state was cleaner: explicit match arm that panicked with 'unexpected close'. Likely caused by compiler warning non-exhaustive after fa1fbffe added Cancelled; the match was patched to add the exhaustive arm just to silence the warning without removing the intent. Pick either: (A) remove pre-assert, restore explicit panic arms, or (B) keep pre-assert, destructure via if-let. Severity: LOW, reviewer round 12, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, tests, cleanup.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:37.214441Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:59.965419Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:59.965100Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4a1860d0: remove unreachable pre-assert and keep explicit Cancelled/Closed panic arms","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-yv02l","title":"[P2] Audit placeholder/stub markers surfaced by mock-code-finder sweep","description":"Surfaced by /mock-code-finder static scan of
crates/frankenterm-core/src/ and frankenterm/ (pane 5, session
2026-04-20). Seven findings; none reach ship-blocking severity, but
each warrants a targeted review to either remove the placeholder,
document the invariant that makes it safe, or replace with a real
implementation.

Categories:
  1. TODO/FIXME/XXX in production
  2. unimplemented!() stubs
  3. placeholder values overwritten later
  4. magic fallback values masking platform/error conditions

Findings:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:242 (cat 4)
  `collect_system_memory` returns `(0, 0)` on non-Linux/non-macOS.
  Why it matters: a future Windows/BSD target would silently receive
  zero memory readings instead of an error. Consider returning
  `Result<(u64, u64), _>` or documenting the platform contract.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs:1473 (cat 1)
  `// TODO(asupersync): replace with asupersync::select! or ...`
  Why it matters: the only surviving prod TODO in frankenterm-core;
  pane 6 owns the asupersync migration, already tracked by
  ft-xbnl0.2.x epic.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/user_preferences.rs:729 (cat 3)
  `action: UserAction::Ignore, // placeholder` inside the synthetic
  trajectory generator. Why it matters: value is overwritten a few
  lines below via `best_action`, but the placeholder is still
  exposed via `obs_base.action` during reward computation between
  lines 730-755. Review whether any feature extractor reads the
  placeholder value.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/vector_index.rs:126 (cat 3)
  `// placeholder for count — will be patched on finish`. Why it
  matters: if the finish path ever takes an early-return before
  patching, the placeholder ships. Add an assertion at finish.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs:980 (cat 3)
  `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder`. Why it matters: same
  pattern as vector_index — review the constructor exit paths.

- frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:4078,4082,4090,4098,4106,4110,4114,
  4141,4144,4150,4158,4161,4164,4170 (cat 2) — 14 `unimplemented!()`
  calls. Why it matters: the cluster needs domain-owner review;
  pane 1 (mux) territory.

- frankenterm/mux/src/pane.rs:609,612,616,624,670,674 (cat 2) — 6
  `unimplemented!()` calls. Why it matters: same cluster as tab.rs,
  pane 1 territory.

No defect shipped from this sweep because the findings are either
low-severity (telemetry fallback on unsupported platforms), tracked
elsewhere (runtime_compat TODO → ft-xbnl0.2.x), or owned by other
panes (mux unimplemented stubs).

Related: in the prior ticks this session I shipped two defensive
fixes for real panic-on-input defects surfaced by similar scans —
0e0668cc (termwiz ReadBuffer out-of-range) and the truncate_for_log
emoji-boundary fix in wait_execution.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:49:35.573939Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:12:56.247438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:12:56.247138Z","close_reason":"Cat-A vector_index fix shipped in 2d522ba7 (parallel agent, functionally identical to my in-progress patch). Cat-B findings validated as benign with documented rationale. Cat-2 mux clusters out-of-scope (pane-1 territory). Full triage table in description.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","code-quality","mock-finder","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-ywlpu","title":"orphan_reaper misparses wezterm cli flag-value forms before subcommand","description":"parse_ps_line_if_reapable() finds the first non-flag token after `cli` and treats it as the subcommand.
That rejects valid invocations like `wezterm cli --config-file /tmp/x.toml list`, where `/tmp/x.toml` is a flag value, not the subcommand.
Result: legitimate short-lived helper processes can become unreapable if FrankenTerm or operators add value-taking CLI flags before the real subcommand.
Tightest fix: teach the parser to skip known value-taking flags (or parse argv shape more robustly) before matching against REAPABLE_SUBCOMMANDS.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:59:49.563841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:23:51.880516Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:23:51.880272Z","close_reason":"Parser now accepts wezterm cli value-taking flags before reapable subcommands; compiled allowlist check fixed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-z01cl","title":"[reality-gap] ft robot checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile families return robot.not_implemented","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:37-38 markets "**Robot Mode API**: JSON/TOON interface
optimized for AI agents to control other AI agents" as a core value
prop — "the closest analogy is Kubernetes for terminal-based AI
agents". This is not a footnote; it's in the opening paragraph.

# Reality (code)

Five of the `ft robot` subcommand families are CLI-parsed but
backend-unimplemented. At `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`:

    :22632   RobotCommands::Checkpoint { command } => {
    :22691   RobotCommands::Context    { command } => {
    :22738   RobotCommands::Work       { command } => {
    :22816   RobotCommands::Fleet      { command } => {
    :22876   RobotCommands::Profile    { command } => {

Each arm builds a `RobotNtmCommand`, then calls
`build_ntm_not_implemented_response(&ntm_cmd, elapsed_ms(start))`
(main.rs:10121) which emits a `robot.not_implemented` envelope with:

    "Command `ft robot <family> <action>` is recognized but not yet
     implemented. This command will be available in a future release.
     NTM equivalent: <command>."

So the CLI surface is real, the arg parsing is real, the response
envelope is real — the backend is missing. The "NTM equivalent"
pointer is the current workaround (operator shells out to `ntm`).

# Scope

Subcommands covered by this gap (15+ concrete actions):

- Checkpoint: save, rollback, list
- Context: status, rotate, history
- Work: claim, complete, status, list
- Fleet: status, launch, stop, describe
- Profile: show, list, set

Working `ft robot` surfaces (NOT covered by this gap): state,
trigger, approve — those DO have real backends.

# Gap severity

- README treats Robot Mode as a pillar feature.
- Users following the opening paragraph to `ft robot fleet launch ...`
  get a `robot.not_implemented` envelope at first invocation.
- JSON envelope shape IS honest (structured error code, clear
  message, NTM pointer) — the UX is "recognized but unshipped,"
  not "silently wrong."
- The honest error makes this a DOCS gap more than a correctness
  bug. Priority is for a README disclosure, not an urgent code fix.

# Recommended disposition

Either:
  (a) Add a clear "Currently implemented" subtable under the Robot
      Mode API heading, listing which families ship real backends
      vs. return `robot.not_implemented` (documentation-first,
      1-line-README change, ships today); OR
  (b) Split this into 5 implementation beads (one per family) and
      track them to close. `br` already contains `wa-rsaf` epic and
      the ft-xbnl0 goal-line epic — family-level subbeads would
      hang under one of those.

(a) is the immediate unblocker; (b) is the real closure path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:10.888900Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:56.189657Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:46:37.162999Z","close_reason":"Landed docs fix (disposition (a)) in 0e1432fc. Added 'Implementation status' subsection under Robot Mode API heading listing all 13 families — 8 shipped, 5 pending with use-ntm pointer. Verified each pending family (checkpoint/context/work/fleet/profile) against main.rs:22632-22879 build_ntm_not_implemented_response routing. The 5 pending implementations themselves remain under wa-rsaf.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","reality-gap","robot"]}
 {"id":"ft-z12h0","title":"Fix frankenterm-core --lib compile blockers in vendored mux client and web_framework","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:35:22.537549Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:11:58.459867Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:11:58.459572Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-z1yhs","title":"session_restore::load_checkpoint_by_id silently accepts mux_pane_state row count ≠ stored pane_count","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:473-481` returns `CheckpointData { pane_count, pane_states }` where:

* `pane_count` is the value stored in `session_checkpoints.pane_count` at save time (stored claim).
* `pane_states` is the rows actually loaded from `mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id = ?1` (actual rows).

Nothing in the load path asserts these match. Line 1204-1205 logs both counts but does not fail on disagreement. Post-ft-ybtyg, the state_hash at `session_checkpoints.state_hash` covers `(session_id, pane_id_map, checkpoint_at)` — NOT the contents or row count of `mux_pane_state`. So:

* Adding a bogus `mux_pane_state` row (checkpoint_id matches) → `pane_states.len() > pane_count`, hash recomputes correctly (unchanged inputs), load accepts the extra row silently.
* Removing a legitimate `mux_pane_state` row → `pane_states.len() < pane_count`, same as above.

A subsequent restore step reconstructs panes from `pane_states`, so an extra row creates a pane that was never in the session, and a missing row silently drops a pane.

# Threat model

DB-write is already fatal, so this is hardening against:
1. Naive filesystem-level tampering (e.g., hostile repo ships a crafted `ft.db` for operator to open).
2. Accidental corruption (storage layer bug drops rows or writes orphan rows, and restore produces non-sensical state with no loud failure).
3. Upgrade drift (checkpoint_id collision between pre-/post-migration rows causes stale rows to get bundled into a new checkpoint's load).

# Fix

In `load_checkpoint_by_id`, after loading `pane_states`, assert `pane_states.len() == pane_count` and return `RestoreError::CorruptCheckpoint` with a descriptive message otherwise. Cost: one integer comparison per load.

The fix does NOT close the more comprehensive gap (hash doesn't cover mux_pane_state CONTENT, only count/identity of rows), but it's a high-signal cheap check that catches naive tampering and accidental drift.

# Regression tests

Two symmetric tests:

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_added_pane_state_row`: create a legit checkpoint, then INSERT an extra `mux_pane_state` row with the same checkpoint_id, call `load_checkpoint_by_id`, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

* `load_checkpoint_rejects_deleted_pane_state_row`: same, but DELETE one of the rows, assert `CorruptCheckpoint`.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no crash, no data loss on the happy path. Silent wrong-answer on the tampered/corrupt path. Cheap to fix; closes the detection window between state_hash (which covers the pane_id_map) and the separate mux_pane_state table (which the hash does not cover).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:38.027641Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693662Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:06.693343Z","close_reason":"Withdrawn: the count-mismatch assertion would false-positive in the real write flow where finalize_restore writes session_checkpoints.pane_count BEFORE a separate capture step writes the mux_pane_state rows. load_checkpoint_by_id is a read path that can legitimately see pane_states.len()=0 while pane_count>0 in that window. Re-examine at a higher layer (is the hash scope wrong? is there a two-phase atomicity guarantee?) rather than the naive row-count check I drafted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -508,7 +508,13 @@ impl TriggerScanner {
         }
     }
 
-    fn for_each_leftmost_match<F>(&self, input: &[u8], mut on_match: F)
+    /// Stream matches via callback, never allocating a result Vec.
+    ///
+    /// Visibility raised to `pub(crate)` for ft-iwlsh — `scan_pipeline.rs`
+    /// previously called the allocating wrapper [`Self::scan_locate`] and
+    /// then iterated and discarded the Vec on every chunk; the callback
+    /// path skips the allocation entirely on the production hot path.
+    pub(crate) fn for_each_leftmost_match<F>(&self, input: &[u8], mut on_match: F)
     where
         F: FnMut(TriggerMatch),
     {
